top_signals.title: Why Cerebras CEO Andrew Feldman Built The World's Largest Computer Chip
top_signals.summary: Bigger means faster in the world of AI chipmaking.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Odd Lots: Why Cerebras Built The Largest Computer Chip (Podcast)
top_signals.summary: Size is the name of the game for the AI chipmaker Cerebras: Their chips are truly massive, about the size of a dinner plate. According to Andrew Feldman, CEO and founder of Cerebras, that is about 58 times larger than the average chip. That sheer size enables blazing fast inference for AI queries. Feldman joins us on the week of his company’s IPO to talk about his core product and how it fits into the AI boom. We discuss the history of the GPU, competition between open-and closed-source models,
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Jess Pegula on the Business of Tennis
top_signals.summary: Alex Rodriguez and Jason Kelly sit down with Jess Pegula to talk about lobbying for change in the business of professional tennis and what she’s learned from her parents. (Source: Bloomberg)
